Cluster ID.name: Identify
Cluster ID.summary: Allows a device to be easily identified, typically through visual or audible means. Supports various IdentifyTypeEnum values for different presentation methods.

revision_history.description: Mandatory global ClusterRevision attribute added
CCB 2808
All Hubs changes
New data model format and notation; add IdentifyType
Removed Query feature

values.name: LightOutput
values.summary: Light output of a lighting product.

values.name: Display
values.summary: Presentation will be visible on display screen.

values.name: Actuator
values.summary: Presentation will be conveyed by actuator functionality such as through a window blind operation or in-wall relay.

values.name: Blink
values.summary: e.g., Light is turned on/off once.

values.name: Breathe
values.summary: e.g., Light is turned on/off over 1 second and repeated 15 times.

values.name: Okay
values.summary: e.g., Colored light turns green for 1 second; non-colored light flashes twice.

values.name: ChannelChange
values.summary: e.g., Colored light turns orange for 8 seconds; non-colored light switches to the maximum brightness for 0.5s and then minimum brightness for 7.5s.

values.name: FinishEffect
values.summary: Complete the current effect sequence before terminating. e.g., if in the middle of a breathe effect (as above), first complete the current 1s breathe effect and then terminate the effect.

values.name: StopEffect
values.summary: Terminate the effect as soon as possible.

values.name: Default
values.summary: Indicates the default effect is used

attributes.name: IdentifyTime
attributes.summary: Remaining identify time in seconds; pseudo: if IdentifyTime > 0 then flash light; on change: decrement every second; if IdentifyTime = 0 then stop flashing

attributes.name: IdentifyType
attributes.summary: Indicates how the identification state is presented

commands.name: Identify
commands.summary: Starts/stops device identification; pseudo: set IdentifyTime := IdentifyTime; affects IdentifyTime

fields.name: IdentifyTime
fields.summary: Sets the IdentifyTime attribute; pseudo: set IdentifyTime := value

commands.effect_on_receipt: set IdentifyTime := IdentifyTime field value; if IdentifyTime > 0 then start identification state; decrement IdentifyTime every second; if IdentifyTime == 0 then terminate identification state

commands.name: TriggerEffect
commands.summary: Triggers a visual effect; pseudo: call triggerEffect(EffectIdentifier, EffectVariant)

fields.name: EffectIdentifier
fields.summary: Identifies the effect to trigger; pseudo: effect := EffectIdentifier

fields.name: EffectVariant
fields.summary: Specifies the variant of the effect; pseudo: variant := EffectVariant

commands.effect_on_receipt: call execute_trigger_effect(EffectIdentifier, EffectVariant); if EffectVariant not supported then call execute_trigger_effect(EffectIdentifier, default_variant)

references.name: ClusterRevision
references.description: Global attr for cluster version; if changed set ClusterRevision := new_version

references.name: Groups Cluster
references.description: Manages group membership; commands affect groups scoped to the accessing fabric

Cluster ID.name: Groups
Cluster ID.summary: Manages the node-wide Group Table per endpoint; supports discovering endpoint membership, adding/removing endpoint from groups; affects groups scoped to the accessing fabric.

revision_history.description: Mandatory global ClusterRevision attribute added; CCB 1745 2100
CCB 2289
New data model format and notation

features.name: GroupNames
features.summary: The ability to store a name for a group; pseudo: if enabled then [group names can be stored]; affects [NameSupport attribute]

values.name: GroupNames
values.summary: The ability to store a name for a group; GroupNames: bit 7 = [ability to store group name]

attributes.name: NameSupport
attributes.summary: Legacy, read-only access to whether Group Names feature is supported; pseudo: attr := FeatureMap[0]; affects GroupNames

commands.name: AddGroup
commands.summary: Adds endpoint to a group; pseudo: call AddGroup(GroupID, GroupName); if [constraint error] then status := CONSTRAINT_ERROR; if [no security material] then status := UNSUPPORTED_ACCESS; affects Group Table

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: Identifies the group; pseudo: groupID := GroupID

fields.name: GroupName
fields.summary: Human-readable name for the group; pseudo: groupName := GroupName

commands.effect_on_receipt: if [command fields not within constraints] then set status := CONSTRAINT_ERROR; if [no security material] then set status := UNSUPPORTED_ACCESS; if [endpoint is member] then update GroupName; set status := SUCCESS; if [no resources] then set status := RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED; add endpoint to group; update GroupName; set status := SUCCESS; if [unicast] then generate AddGroupResponse

commands.name: ViewGroup
commands.summary: Requests group name for a group; pseudo: call ViewGroup(GroupID); if [endpoint is member] then status := SUCCESS; else status := NOT_FOUND; affects Group Table

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: Identifies the group; pseudo: groupID := GroupID

commands.effect_on_receipt: if [command fields not within constraints] then set status := CONSTRAINT_ERROR; if [endpoint is member] then set status := SUCCESS; else set status := NOT_FOUND; if [unicast] then generate ViewGroupResponse

commands.name: GetGroupMembership
commands.summary: Inquires about group membership; pseudo: call GetGroupMembership(GroupList); responds with group IDs; affects Group Table

fields.name: GroupList
fields.summary: List of group IDs to check; pseudo: groupList := GroupList

commands.effect_on_receipt: if [GroupList is empty] then respond with all group IDs; if [GroupList contains member] then respond with matching group IDs; if [GroupList contains no member] then if [unicast] then respond with empty GroupList

commands.name: RemoveGroup
commands.summary: Removes endpoint from a group; pseudo: call RemoveGroup(GroupID); if [endpoint is member] then remove membership; affects Group Table

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: Identifies the group to remove; pseudo: groupID := GroupID

commands.effect_on_receipt: if [command fields not within constraints] then set status := CONSTRAINT_ERROR; if [endpoint is member] then remove membership; set status := SUCCESS; else set status := NOT_FOUND; if [unicast] then generate RemoveGroupResponse; if [Scenes Management supported] then remove scenes associated with group

commands.name: RemoveAllGroups
commands.summary: Removes all group associations; pseudo: call RemoveAllGroups(); removes all memberships; affects Group Table
commands.effect_on_receipt: remove all group memberships; if [unicast and response not suppressed] then generate response with status := SUCCESS; if [Scenes Management supported] then remove all scenes except those associated with group ID 0

commands.name: AddGroupIfIdentifying
commands.summary: Adds endpoint to a group if identifying; pseudo: call AddGroupIfIdentifying(GroupID, GroupName); if [is identifying] then add to group; affects Group Table

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: Identifies the group; pseudo: groupID := GroupID

fields.name: GroupName
fields.summary: Human-readable name for the group; pseudo: groupName := GroupName

commands.effect_on_receipt: if [server not identifying] then set status := SUCCESS; if [command fields not within constraints] then set status := CONSTRAINT_ERROR; if [no security material] then set status := UNSUPPORTED_ACCESS; if [endpoint is member] then set status := SUCCESS; if [no resources] then set status := RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED; add endpoint to group; update GroupName; set status := SUCCESS; if [unicast and status != SUCCESS or unicast and status == SUCCESS and response not suppressed] then generate response with status

commands.name: AddGroupResponse
commands.summary: Response to AddGroup command; pseudo: return status, groupID

fields.name: Status
fields.summary: Status of the AddGroup command; pseudo: status := status

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: GroupID of the AddGroup command; pseudo: groupID := groupID

commands.name: ViewGroupResponse
commands.summary: Response to ViewGroup command; pseudo: return status, groupID, groupName

fields.name: Status
fields.summary: Status of the ViewGroup command; pseudo: status := status

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: GroupID of the ViewGroup command; pseudo: groupID := groupID

fields.name: GroupName
fields.summary: GroupName of the ViewGroup command; pseudo: groupName := groupName

commands.name: GetGroupMembershipResponse
commands.summary: Response to GetGroupMembership command; pseudo: return capacity, groupList

fields.name: Capacity
fields.summary: Remaining capacity of the Group Table; pseudo: capacity := capacity

fields.name: GroupList
fields.summary: List of group IDs; pseudo: groupList := groupList

commands.name: RemoveGroupResponse
commands.summary: Response to RemoveGroup command; pseudo: return status, groupID

fields.name: Status
fields.summary: Status of the RemoveGroup command; pseudo: status := status

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: GroupID of the RemoveGroup command; pseudo: groupID := groupID

references.name: ClusterRevision
references.description: Global attr for cluster version; set [ClusterRevision] := [new version]

references.name: Scenes Management cluster
references.description: Removal of scenes associated with groups on RemoveGroup and RemoveAllGroups commands

Cluster ID.name: Scenes Management
Cluster ID.summary: Manages scenes, allowing to store and recall device configurations. Supports storing scene names if SN feature is enabled.

revision_history.description: Based on ZCL Scenes Cluster; Cluster ID := 0x0062; name := Scenes Management; removed provisional status; removed SceneCount, CurrentScene, CurrentGroup, SceneValid, NameSupport; removed Explicit, TableSize, FabricScenes features

Classification.summary: Affects endpoint behavior by managing scene configurations.

features.name: SceneNames
features.summary: The ability to store a name for a scene. pseudo: if enabled then [scene names can be stored]; affects [scene name storage]

Dependencies.title: Scenes Management requires Groups
Dependencies.trigger_condition: Endpoint implements Scenes Management server cluster
Dependencies.effect: Endpoint SHALL also implement the Groups server cluster
Dependencies.summary: If an endpoint implements the Scenes Management server cluster, it must also implement the Groups server cluster. This ensures that scene operations can correctly manage group memberships. if (ScenesManagementServer) {GroupsServer = true;} else {GroupsServer = false;}

Dependencies.title: Groups commands affect scenes
Dependencies.trigger_condition: RemoveGroup or RemoveAllGroups command is called in Groups cluster
Dependencies.pseudocode: if RemoveGroup OR RemoveAllGroups then RemoveScenes
Dependencies.summary: When RemoveGroup or RemoveAllGroups is called in the Groups cluster, the corresponding scenes associated with those groups are also removed from the Scenes Management cluster. if (RemoveGroup || RemoveAllGroups) {RemoveScenes();} else { /* no action */ }

Dependencies.title: Fabric Scoping for Attributes and Commands
Dependencies.trigger_condition: Attribute read/write or command invoked without accessing fabric
Dependencies.summary: If an attribute read, write, or command is invoked on the server without an accessing fabric, the operation will fail and return an UNSUPPORTED_ACCESS status code to the client. if (!fabric) {return UNSUPPORTED_ACCESS;} else {/* proceed */}

Dependencies.title: Scene data removal on fabric removal
Dependencies.trigger_condition: Leaving a fabric with RemoveFabric command
Dependencies.effect: All scenes data for the associated fabric is removed from the Scene Table
Dependencies.summary: Upon leaving a fabric using the RemoveFabric command of the Operational Credentials Cluster, all scene data associated with that fabric is removed from the Scene Table. if (RemoveFabric) {RemoveSceneData();} else {/* no action */}

Handling of fabric-scoping.rule: Attributes and commands are scoped to accessing fabric only
Operations without an accessing fabric SHALL fail
Handling of fabric-scoping.pseudocode: if accessing_fabric == null then return UNSUPPORTED_ACCESS

Handling of fabric-scoping.rule: Scenes with same Group ID + Scene ID across fabrics are fabric-isolated
Removing a fabric clears associated scenes
Scene Table capacity per fabric < half of SceneTableSize (max 253)
Handling of fabric-scoping.pseudocode: if scene_table[fabric].count >= floor(SceneTableSize/2) or scene_table[fabric].count > 253 then apply resource_exhaustion_behavior()

values.name: CopyAllScenes
values.summary: Copy all scenes in the scene table; pseudo: bit = [meaning]

values.name: AttributeValueList
values.summary: list[AttributeValuePairStruct]; pseudo: AttributeValueList = [list of attribute value pairs]

attributes.name: LastConfiguredBy
attributes.summary: Node ID that last configured the Scene Table; null if not configured; scoped to accessing fabric

attributes.name: SceneTableSize
attributes.summary: Number of entries in the Scene Table on this endpoint; total across all fabrics; min size 16

attributes.name: FabricSceneInfo
attributes.summary: List of fabric scoped information about scenes on this endpoint; number of entries <= number of supported fabrics

commands.name: AddScene
commands.summary: Adds a scene to the Scene Table; pseudo: call AddScene(GroupID, SceneID, TransitionTime, SceneName, ExtensionFieldSetStructs); affects SceneTable

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: Group identifier in the Group Table; pseudo: set group_id := GroupID

fields.name: SceneID
fields.summary: Scene identifier in the Scene Table; pseudo: set scene_id := SceneID

fields.name: TransitionTime
fields.summary: Transition time of the scene in milliseconds; pseudo: set transition_time := TransitionTime

fields.name: SceneName
fields.summary: Name of the scene; pseudo: set scene_name := SceneName

fields.name: ExtensionFieldSetStructs
fields.summary: List of extension fields; pseudo: for each field: set attribute := value

commands.effect_on_receipt: if GroupID != 0 then if GroupID not in Group Table then set status := INVALID_COMMAND else continue; if ExtensionFieldSetStructs invalid then set status := INVALID_COMMAND else continue; if scene exists then replace scene else if no capacity then set status := RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED else add scene; set SceneTransitionTime := TransitionTime; if unicast then call AddSceneResponse(status)

commands.name: AddSceneResponse
commands.summary: Response to AddScene command; pseudo: return status, GroupID, SceneID

fields.name: Status
fields.summary: Status of the AddScene command; pseudo: set status := status

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: Group identifier from the AddScene command; pseudo: set group_id := GroupID

fields.name: SceneID
fields.summary: Scene identifier from the AddScene command; pseudo: set scene_id := SceneID

commands.name: ViewScene
commands.summary: Views a scene from the Scene Table; pseudo: call ViewScene(GroupID, SceneID); affects SceneTable

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: Group identifier in the Group Table; pseudo: set group_id := GroupID

fields.name: SceneID
fields.summary: Scene identifier in the Scene Table; pseudo: set scene_id := SceneID

commands.effect_on_receipt: if GroupID != 0 then if GroupID not in Group Table then set status := INVALID_COMMAND else continue; if scene (GroupID, SceneID) not in Scene Table then set status := NOT_FOUND else set status := SUCCESS; if unicast then call ViewSceneResponse(status, scene)

commands.name: ViewSceneResponse
commands.summary: Response to ViewScene command; pseudo: return status, GroupID, SceneID, TransitionTime, SceneName, ExtensionFieldSetStructs

fields.name: Status
fields.summary: Status of the ViewScene command; pseudo: set status := status

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: Group identifier from the ViewScene command; pseudo: set group_id := GroupID

fields.name: SceneID
fields.summary: Scene identifier from the ViewScene command; pseudo: set scene_id := SceneID

fields.name: TransitionTime
fields.summary: Transition time of the scene; pseudo: set transition_time := TransitionTime

fields.name: SceneName
fields.summary: Name of the scene; pseudo: set scene_name := SceneName

fields.name: ExtensionFieldSetStructs
fields.summary: List of extension fields; pseudo: for each field: set attribute := value

commands.name: RemoveScene
commands.summary: Removes a scene from the Scene Table; pseudo: call RemoveScene(GroupID, SceneID); affects SceneTable

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: Group identifier in the Group Table; pseudo: set group_id := GroupID

fields.name: SceneID
fields.summary: Scene identifier in the Scene Table; pseudo: set scene_id := SceneID

commands.effect_on_receipt: if GroupID != 0 then if GroupID not in Group Table then set status := INVALID_COMMAND else continue; if scene (GroupID, SceneID) not in Scene Table then set status := NOT_FOUND else remove scene; set status := SUCCESS; if unicast then call RemoveSceneResponse(status)

commands.name: RemoveSceneResponse
commands.summary: Response to RemoveScene command; pseudo: return status, GroupID, SceneID

fields.name: Status
fields.summary: Status of the RemoveScene command; pseudo: set status := status

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: Group identifier from the RemoveScene command; pseudo: set group_id := GroupID

fields.name: SceneID
fields.summary: Scene identifier from the RemoveScene command; pseudo: set scene_id := SceneID

commands.name: RemoveAllScenes
commands.summary: Removes all scenes for a group from the Scene Table; pseudo: call RemoveAllScenes(GroupID); affects SceneTable

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: Group identifier in the Group Table; pseudo: set group_id := GroupID

commands.effect_on_receipt: if GroupID != 0 then if GroupID not in Group Table then set status := INVALID_COMMAND else continue; remove all scenes for GroupID; set status := SUCCESS; if unicast then call RemoveAllScenesResponse(status)

commands.name: RemoveAllScenesResponse
commands.summary: Response to RemoveAllScenes command; pseudo: return status, GroupID

fields.name: Status
fields.summary: Status of the RemoveAllScenes command; pseudo: set status := status

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: Group identifier from the RemoveAllScenes command; pseudo: set group_id := GroupID

commands.name: StoreScene
commands.summary: Stores the current state as a scene in the Scene Table; pseudo: call StoreScene(GroupID, SceneID); affects SceneTable

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: Group identifier in the Group Table; pseudo: set group_id := GroupID

fields.name: SceneID
fields.summary: Scene identifier in the Scene Table; pseudo: set scene_id := SceneID

commands.effect_on_receipt: if GroupID != 0 then if GroupID not in Group Table then set status := INVALID_COMMAND else continue; if scene exists then replace ExtensionFieldSets else if no capacity then set status := RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED else add new entry with current state; set status := SUCCESS; if unicast then call StoreSceneResponse(status)

commands.name: StoreSceneResponse
commands.summary: Response to StoreScene command; pseudo: return status, GroupID, SceneID

fields.name: Status
fields.summary: Status of the StoreScene command; pseudo: set status := status

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: Group identifier from the StoreScene command; pseudo: set group_id := GroupID

fields.name: SceneID
fields.summary: Scene identifier from the StoreScene command; pseudo: set scene_id := SceneID

commands.name: RecallScene
commands.summary: Recalls a scene from the Scene Table; pseudo: call RecallScene(GroupID, SceneID, TransitionTime); affects attributes of other clusters

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: Group identifier in the Group Table; pseudo: set group_id := GroupID

fields.name: SceneID
fields.summary: Scene identifier in the Scene Table; pseudo: set scene_id := SceneID

fields.name: TransitionTime
fields.summary: Transition time of the scene in milliseconds; pseudo: set transition_time := TransitionTime

commands.effect_on_receipt: if GroupID != 0 then if GroupID not in Group Table then set status := INVALID_COMMAND else continue; if scene (GroupID, SceneID) not in Scene Table then set status := NOT_FOUND else retrieve scene; for each cluster: retrieve extension field sets and set attributes; if no extension field set then cluster state unchanged; if field set omits attributes then attributes unchanged; if unknown attribute then skip; set status := SUCCESS; if unicast then generate response(status)

commands.name: GetSceneMembership
commands.summary: Gets the scene membership for a group; pseudo: call GetSceneMembership(GroupID); affects SceneTable

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: Group identifier in the Group Table; pseudo: set group_id := GroupID

commands.effect_on_receipt: if GroupID != 0 then if GroupID not in Group Table then set status := INVALID_COMMAND else continue; set status := SUCCESS; if unicast then call GetSceneMembershipResponse(status)

commands.name: GetSceneMembershipResponse
commands.summary: Response to GetSceneMembership command; pseudo: return status, Capacity, GroupID, SceneList

fields.name: Status
fields.summary: Status of the GetSceneMembership command; pseudo: set status := status

fields.name: Capacity
fields.summary: Remaining capacity of the Scene Table; pseudo: set capacity := capacity

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: Group identifier from the GetSceneMembership command; pseudo: set group_id := GroupID

fields.name: SceneList
fields.summary: List of scene identifiers in the Scene Table; pseudo: set scene_list := SceneList

commands.name: CopyScene
commands.summary: Copies scenes from one group/scene to another; pseudo: call CopyScene(Mode, GroupIdentifierFrom, SceneIdentifierFrom, GroupIdentifierTo, SceneIdentifierTo); affects SceneTable

fields.name: Mode
fields.summary: Information of how the scene copy is to proceed; pseudo: set mode := Mode

fields.name: GroupIdentifierFrom
fields.summary: Identifier of the group from which the scene is to be copied; pseudo: set group_id_from := GroupIdentifierFrom

fields.name: SceneIdentifierFrom
fields.summary: Identifier of the scene from which the scene is to be copied; pseudo: set scene_id_from := SceneIdentifierFrom

fields.name: GroupIdentifierTo
fields.summary: Identifier of the group to which the scene is to be copied; pseudo: set group_id_to := GroupIdentifierTo

fields.name: SceneIdentifierTo
fields.summary: Identifier of the scene to which the scene is to be copied; pseudo: set scene_id_to := SceneIdentifierTo

commands.effect_on_receipt: if GroupIdentifierFrom != 0 or GroupIdentifierTo != 0 then if GroupIdentifierFrom or GroupIdentifierTo not in Group Table then set status := INVALID_COMMAND else continue; if CopyAllScenes == 0 then if scene (GroupIdentifierFrom, SceneIdentifierFrom) not in Scene Table then set status := NOT_FOUND else continue; if CopyAllScenes == 1 then copy all scenes with group identifier equal to GroupIdentifierFrom to GroupIdentifierTo; if CopyAllScenes == 0 then copy scene (GroupIdentifierFrom, SceneIdentifierFrom) to scene (GroupIdentifierTo, SceneIdentifierTo); if scene exists then replace scene; if no capacity then set status := RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED; set status := SUCCESS; if unicast then call CopySceneResponse(status)

commands.name: CopySceneResponse
commands.summary: Response to CopyScene command; pseudo: return status, GroupIdentifierFrom, SceneIdentifierFrom

fields.name: Status
fields.summary: Status of the CopyScene command; pseudo: set status := status

fields.name: GroupIdentifierFrom
fields.summary: Group identifier from the CopyScene command; pseudo: set group_id_from := GroupIdentifierFrom

fields.name: SceneIdentifierFrom
fields.summary: Scene identifier from the CopyScene command; pseudo: set scene_id_from := SceneIdentifierFrom

references.name: ClusterRevision
references.description: Global attr for cluster version (not defined here)

references.name: Groups server cluster
references.description: Dependency cluster; RemoveGroup/RemoveAllGroups commands also remove scenes

references.name: Scene Table Extensions
references.description: Scene Table Extension SHALL only use attributes with the Scene quality

references.name: ExtensionFieldSetStruct
references.description: Holds a set of values of scene attributes for a cluster on the same endpoint

references.name: Group Table
references.description: Used for GroupID validation; if GroupID not in Group Table then set status := INVALID_COMMAND

Cluster ID.name: On/Off
Cluster ID.summary: Manages on/off state of a device. If Level Control command sets OnOff to FALSE, OnTime := 0. If OnOff to TRUE and OnTime = 0, OffWaitTime := 0. Supports Lighting and DeadFrontBehavior features.

revision_history.description: Mandatory global ClusterRevision attribute added; CCB 1555
ZLO 1.0: StartUpOnOff
FeatureMap global attribute support with Level Control and Lighting feature
New data model format and notation
Addition of Dead Front behavior and associated FeatureMap entry
Addition of OffOnly feature and associated FeatureMap entry

Classification.summary: Basic on/off control functionality; affects endpoint.

features.name: Lighting
features.summary: Behavior that supports lighting applications; pseudo: if enabled then [lighting behavior]; affects [OnTime, OffWaitTime]

features.name: DeadFrontBehavior
features.summary: Device has Dead Front behavior; pseudo: if enabled and OnOff is FALSE then [other clusters may fail or change attributes]; affects [other cluster attributes]

features.name: OffOnly
features.summary: Device supports the OffOnly feature; pseudo: if enabled then [Off command supported, On/Toggle commands not supported]; affects [command availability]

values.name: AcceptOnlyWhenOn
values.summary: Indicates a command is only accepted when in On state.

values.name: Off
values.summary: Set the OnOff attribute to FALSE; pseudo: OnOff = FALSE

values.name: On
values.summary: Set the OnOff attribute to TRUE; pseudo: OnOff = TRUE

values.name: Toggle
values.summary: If the previous value of the OnOff attribute is equal to FALSE, set the OnOff attribute to TRUE. If the previous value of the OnOff attribute is equal to TRUE, set the OnOff attribute to FALSE (toggle).

values.name: DelayedOffSlowFade
values.summary: 50% dim down in 0.8 seconds then fade to off in 12 seconds

values.name: DyingLightFadeOff
values.summary: 20% dim up in 0.5s then fade to off in 1 second

attributes.name: OnOff
attributes.summary: Indicates if device is on (TRUE) or off (FALSE); on Off cmd: attr := FALSE; on On cmd: attr := TRUE; on Toggle cmd: attr := !attr

attributes.name: GlobalSceneControl
attributes.summary: Prevents storing all-off state as global scene; after On cmd: attr := TRUE; after OffWithEffect: attr := FALSE; affects global scene recall

attributes.name: OnTime
attributes.summary: Time (in 1/10s) to maintain On state before auto-off; on OnWithTimedOff: attr := [value]; affects auto-off timing

attributes.name: OffWaitTime
attributes.summary: Time (in 1/10s) to guard Off state from re-On; on OnWithTimedOff: attr := [value]; affects delayed-off timing

attributes.name: StartUpOnOff
attributes.summary: Defines startup behavior; on power-up: if attr != null then OnOff := [value]; affects initial OnOff state

commands.name: Off
commands.summary: Sets OnOff to FALSE; pseudo: set OnOff := FALSE; affects OnOff
commands.effect_on_receipt: set OnOff := FALSE; if OnTime is supported then set OnTime := 0

commands.name: On
commands.summary: Sets OnOff to TRUE if OffOnly is not supported; pseudo: if !OffOnly then set OnOff := TRUE; affects OnOff
commands.effect_on_receipt: if OffOnly is supported then send UNSUPPORTED_COMMAND else set OnOff := TRUE; if OnTime is supported and OffWaitTime is supported then if OnTime = 0 then set OffWaitTime := 0

commands.name: Toggle
commands.summary: Toggles OnOff; pseudo: if OnOff then set OnOff := FALSE else set OnOff := TRUE; affects OnOff
commands.effect_on_receipt: if OffOnly is supported then send UNSUPPORTED_COMMAND else if OnOff = FALSE then set OnOff := TRUE else set OnOff := FALSE; if OnTime is supported and OffWaitTime is supported then if OnOff = FALSE and OnTime = 0 then set OffWaitTime := 0; if OnOff = TRUE then set OnTime := 0

commands.name: OffWithEffect
commands.summary: Turns off with fading effects; pseudo: if GlobalSceneControl then store settings; set OnOff := FALSE; affects OnOff

fields.name: EffectIdentifier
fields.summary: Specifies the fading effect; pseudo: select effect from EffectIdentifierEnum

fields.name: EffectVariant
fields.summary: Variant of the effect; pseudo: select variant from DelayedAllOffEffectVariantEnum or DyingLightEffectVariantEnum

commands.effect_on_receipt: if GlobalSceneControl = TRUE then call store_settings(); set GlobalSceneControl := FALSE; set OnOff := FALSE; if OnTime is supported then set OnTime := 0 else set OnOff := FALSE

commands.name: OnWithRecallGlobalScene
commands.summary: Recalls settings when device was turned off; pseudo: call recall_global_scene(); set GlobalSceneControl := TRUE; affects OnOff
commands.effect_on_receipt: if GlobalSceneControl = TRUE then discard command else call recall_global_scene(); set GlobalSceneControl := TRUE; if OnTime is supported and OffWaitTime is supported then if OnTime = 0 then set OffWaitTime := 0

commands.name: OnWithTimedOff
commands.summary: Turns on for a specific duration; pseudo: set OnTime := max(OnTime, X); set OffWaitTime := Y; set OnOff := TRUE; affects OnTime, OffWaitTime, OnOff

fields.name: OnOffControl
fields.summary: Controls command processing; pseudo: check AcceptOnlyWhenOn bit

fields.name: OnTime
fields.summary: Adjusts OnTime attribute; pseudo: set OnTime := X

fields.name: OffWaitTime
fields.summary: Adjusts OffWaitTime attribute; pseudo: set OffWaitTime := Y

commands.effect_on_receipt: if AcceptOnlyWhenOn = 1 and OnOff = FALSE then discard command; if OffWaitTime > 0 and OnOff = FALSE then set OffWaitTime := min(OffWaitTime, OffWaitTime); else set OnTime := max(OnTime, OnTime); set OffWaitTime := OffWaitTime; set OnOff := TRUE; if OnTime != 0xFFFF and OffWaitTime != 0xFFFF then repeat every 0.1s: if OnOff = TRUE and OnTime > 0 then decrement OnTime; if OnTime = 0 then set OffWaitTime := 0; set OnOff := FALSE; if OnOff = FALSE and OffWaitTime > 0 then decrement OffWaitTime; if OffWaitTime = 0 then terminate update

State Description.behavior: OnTime decremented every 1/10s unless 0xFFFF. When OnTime reaches 0, OffWaitTime and OnOff are set to 0 and FALSE respectively.
State Description.pseudocode: if OnTime != 0xFFFF then every 100ms: OnTime := OnTime - 1; if OnTime == 0 then OffWaitTime := 0; OnOff := FALSE

State Description.behavior: OffWaitTime decremented every 1/10s unless 0xFFFF. When OffWaitTime reaches 0, the update terminates and OnOff remains FALSE.
State Description.pseudocode: if OffWaitTime != 0xFFFF then every 100ms: OffWaitTime := OffWaitTime - 1; if OffWaitTime == 0 then terminate

references.name: ClusterRevision
references.description: Global attr for cluster version; value SHALL be highest revision number

references.name: Level Control
references.description: Level Control cluster command; if OnOff is FALSE then set OnTime := 0

references.name: Scenes Cluster
references.description: Scene storage for scene attrs (if mentioned)

Cluster ID.name: Level Control
Cluster ID.summary: Manages device level control. It is recommended to derive another cluster to better define the application and domain requirements.

revision_history.description: Mandatory global ClusterRevision attribute added
Added Options attribute, state change table; ZLO 1.0; Base cluster (no change)
FeatureMap support with On/Off, Lighting and Frequency features
New data model format and notation
Added Q quality for CurrentLevel, CurrentFrequency and RemainingTime attributes, added clarifications for behavior related to Step value and adjusted constraint of DefaultMoveRate

Classification.summary: Base application cluster affecting endpoint level control.

features.name: OnOff
features.summary: Dependency with the On/Off cluster; pseudo: if OnOff cluster is present then [enable OnOff feature]; affects [OnOff interaction]

features.name: Lighting
features.summary: Behavior that supports lighting applications; pseudo: if enabled then [adjust lighting params]; affects [CurrentLevel behavior]

features.name: Frequency
features.summary: Supports frequency attributes and behavior; pseudo: if enabled then [enable frequency control]; affects [frequency attributes]

values.name: ExecuteIfOff
values.summary: Dependency on On/Off cluster; pseudo: bit = [dependency]

values.name: CoupleColorTempToLevel
values.summary: Dependency on Color Control cluster; pseudo: bit = [dependency]

attributes.name: CurrentLevel
attributes.summary: Indicates the current level of the device; on change: reportable <= 1/sec, at end of transition, or null <-> value

attributes.name: RemainingTime
attributes.summary: Time remaining until command complete (1/10s); on change: reportable if > 10 or delta > 10 or to 0

attributes.name: MinLevel
attributes.summary: Minimum value of CurrentLevel

attributes.name: MinLevel
attributes.summary: Minimum value of CurrentLevel

attributes.name: MaxLevel
attributes.summary: Maximum value of CurrentLevel

attributes.name: CurrentFrequency
attributes.summary: Frequency at which device is at CurrentLevel; on change: reportable <= 1/sec, at start/end of transition

attributes.name: MinFrequency
attributes.summary: Minimum value of CurrentFrequency

attributes.name: MaxFrequency
attributes.summary: Maximum value of CurrentFrequency

attributes.name: OnOffTransitionTime
attributes.summary: Time to move to/from target level when On/Off commands received (1/10s)

attributes.name: OnLevel
attributes.summary: Value CurrentLevel set to when OnOff is TRUE

attributes.name: OnTransitionTime
attributes.summary: Time to move from min to max level when On command received (1/10s)

attributes.name: OffTransitionTime
attributes.summary: Time to move from max to min level when Off command received (1/10s)

attributes.name: DefaultMoveRate
attributes.summary: Movement rate (units/sec) when Move command received with null Rate

attributes.name: Options
attributes.summary: Selected options of the device; pseudo: if OnOff is FALSE and ExecuteIfOff is 0 then command := ignore

attributes.name: StartUpCurrentLevel
attributes.summary: Desired startup level; pseudo: if value = 0 then CurrentLevel := min; if value = null then CurrentLevel := previous; else CurrentLevel := value

commands.name: MoveToLevel
commands.summary: Moves to a specified level; pseudo: call moveToLevel(level, transitionTime, options); affects CurrentLevel; if transitionTime is null, use OnOffTransitionTime

fields.name: Level
fields.summary: Target level to move to; pseudo: CurrentLevel := Level

fields.name: TransitionTime
fields.summary: Time to reach target level (tenths of a second); pseudo: duration := TransitionTime * 0.1

fields.name: OptionsMask
fields.summary: Mask for options bitmap

fields.name: OptionsOverride
fields.summary: Override for options bitmap

commands.effect_on_receipt: tempOptions := Options; if OptionsMask is present then for each bit in OptionsMask: if bit is 1 then tempOptions[bit] := OptionsOverride[bit]; set Options := tempOptions; set CurrentLevel := Level; if TransitionTime is null then if OnOffTransitionTime is present then duration := OnOffTransitionTime else duration := as fast as possible else duration := TransitionTime; start_timer(duration)

commands.name: Move
commands.summary: Moves continuously up or down; pseudo: call move(moveMode, rate, options); affects CurrentLevel; if rate is null, use DefaultMoveRate

fields.name: MoveMode
fields.summary: Direction of movement (up or down)

fields.name: Rate
fields.summary: Rate of movement (units per second)

fields.name: OptionsMask
fields.summary: Mask for options bitmap

fields.name: OptionsOverride
fields.summary: Override for options bitmap

commands.effect_on_receipt: if Rate == 0 then return INVALID_COMMAND; tempOptions := Options; if OptionsMask is present then for each bit in OptionsMask: if bit is 1 then tempOptions[bit] := OptionsOverride[bit]; set Options := tempOptions; if Rate is null then if DefaultMoveRate is supported then Rate := DefaultMoveRate else Rate := as fast as possible; if MoveMode == Up then increase CurrentLevel at Rate until max level reached else decrease CurrentLevel at Rate until min level reached

commands.name: Step
commands.summary: Steps up or down by a specified amount; pseudo: call step(stepMode, stepSize, transitionTime, options); affects CurrentLevel

fields.name: StepMode
fields.summary: Direction of step (up or down)

fields.name: StepSize
fields.summary: Amount to step by

fields.name: TransitionTime
fields.summary: Time to perform the step (tenths of a second)

fields.name: OptionsMask
fields.summary: Mask for options bitmap

fields.name: OptionsOverride
fields.summary: Override for options bitmap

commands.effect_on_receipt: if StepSize == 0 then return INVALID_COMMAND; tempOptions := Options; if OptionsMask is present then for each bit in OptionsMask: if bit is 1 then tempOptions[bit] := OptionsOverride[bit]; set Options := tempOptions; if StepMode == Up then if CurrentLevel + StepSize > maxLevel then CurrentLevel := maxLevel; transitionTime := proportional reduction else CurrentLevel := CurrentLevel + StepSize; else if CurrentLevel - StepSize < minLevel then CurrentLevel := minLevel; transitionTime := proportional reduction else CurrentLevel := CurrentLevel - StepSize; start_timer(transitionTime)

commands.name: Stop
commands.summary: Stops any ongoing move, step, or move-to-level command; pseudo: call stop(); affects CurrentLevel, RemainingTime

fields.name: OptionsMask
fields.summary: Mask for options bitmap

fields.name: OptionsOverride
fields.summary: Override for options bitmap

commands.effect_on_receipt: tempOptions := Options; if OptionsMask is present then for each bit in OptionsMask: if bit is 1 then tempOptions[bit] := OptionsOverride[bit]; set Options := tempOptions; stop_timer(); RemainingTime := 0

commands.name: MoveToLevelWithOnOff
commands.summary: Moves to a specified level and controls On/Off; pseudo: call moveToLevelWithOnOff(level, transitionTime, options); affects CurrentLevel, OnOff

fields.name: Level
fields.summary: Target level to move to

fields.name: TransitionTime
fields.summary: Time to reach target level (tenths of a second)

fields.name: OptionsMask
fields.summary: Mask for options bitmap

fields.name: OptionsOverride
fields.summary: Override for options bitmap

commands.effect_on_receipt: tempOptions := Options; if OptionsMask is present then for each bit in OptionsMask: if bit is 1 then tempOptions[bit] := OptionsOverride[bit]; set Options := tempOptions; if Level > minLevel then set OnOff := TRUE else set OnOff := FALSE; set CurrentLevel := Level; if TransitionTime is null then if OnOffTransitionTime is present then duration := OnOffTransitionTime else duration := as fast as possible else duration := TransitionTime; start_timer(duration)

commands.name: MoveWithOnOff
commands.summary: Moves continuously up or down and controls On/Off; pseudo: call moveWithOnOff(moveMode, rate, options); affects CurrentLevel, OnOff

fields.name: MoveMode
fields.summary: Direction of movement (up or down)

fields.name: Rate
fields.summary: Rate of movement (units per second)

fields.name: OptionsMask
fields.summary: Mask for options bitmap

fields.name: OptionsOverride
fields.summary: Override for options bitmap

commands.effect_on_receipt: if Rate == 0 then return INVALID_COMMAND; tempOptions := Options; if OptionsMask is present then for each bit in OptionsMask: if bit is 1 then tempOptions[bit] := OptionsOverride[bit]; set Options := tempOptions; if Rate is null then if DefaultMoveRate is supported then Rate := DefaultMoveRate else Rate := as fast as possible; if MoveMode == Up then set OnOff := TRUE; increase CurrentLevel at Rate until max level reached else set OnOff := FALSE; decrease CurrentLevel at Rate until min level reached

commands.name: StepWithOnOff
commands.summary: Steps up or down by a specified amount and controls On/Off; pseudo: call stepWithOnOff(stepMode, stepSize, transitionTime, options); affects CurrentLevel, OnOff

fields.name: StepMode
fields.summary: Direction of step (up or down)

fields.name: StepSize
fields.summary: Amount to step by

fields.name: TransitionTime
fields.summary: Time to perform the step (tenths of a second)

fields.name: OptionsMask
fields.summary: Mask for options bitmap

fields.name: OptionsOverride
fields.summary: Override for options bitmap

commands.effect_on_receipt: if StepSize == 0 then return INVALID_COMMAND; tempOptions := Options; if OptionsMask is present then for each bit in OptionsMask: if bit is 1 then tempOptions[bit] := OptionsOverride[bit]; set Options := tempOptions; if StepMode == Up then set OnOff := TRUE; if CurrentLevel + StepSize > maxLevel then CurrentLevel := maxLevel; transitionTime := proportional reduction else CurrentLevel := CurrentLevel + StepSize; else set OnOff := FALSE; if CurrentLevel - StepSize < minLevel then CurrentLevel := minLevel; transitionTime := proportional reduction else CurrentLevel := CurrentLevel - StepSize; start_timer(transitionTime)

commands.name: StopWithOnOff
commands.summary: Stops any ongoing move, step, or move-to-level command and controls On/Off; pseudo: call stopWithOnOff(); affects CurrentLevel, RemainingTime

fields.name: OptionsMask
fields.summary: Mask for options bitmap

fields.name: OptionsOverride
fields.summary: Override for options bitmap

commands.effect_on_receipt: tempOptions := Options; if OptionsMask is present then for each bit in OptionsMask: if bit is 1 then tempOptions[bit] := OptionsOverride[bit]; set Options := tempOptions; stop_timer(); RemainingTime := 0

commands.name: MoveToClosestFrequency
commands.summary: Moves to the closest supported frequency; pseudo: call moveToClosestFrequency(frequency); affects CurrentFrequency

fields.name: Frequency
fields.summary: Requested frequency

commands.effect_on_receipt: if device can approximate Frequency then set CurrentFrequency := Frequency else return CONSTRAINT_ERROR

State Change Table for Lighting.summary: MoveToLevel ignored when Off and EiO=0

result.output: Turns on and output level adjusts or stays at half

State Change Table for Lighting.summary: MoveToLevelWithOnOff turns on device and adjusts brightness

result.output: Turns on and output level adjusts to or stays at half

State Change Table for Lighting.summary: MoveToLevelWithOnOff turns on device and adjusts brightness when EiO=1
Move ignored when Off and EiO=1

result.output: Turn on and output level adjusts to or stays at full

State Change Table for Lighting.summary: MoveWithOnOff turns on device and adjusts brightness when EiO=1
MoveWithOnOff to min level when Off and EiO=1

result.output: Output level adjusts to or stays at half
Output level adjusts to or stays at full

State Change Table for Lighting.summary: Move to min level when On
MoveWithOnOff to min level turns Off

references.name: ClusterRevision
references.description: Global attr for cluster version; SHALL be highest revision number

references.name: On/Off
references.description: On/Off cluster; Dependency with the On/Off cluster

references.name: Scenes Cluster
references.description: Scene storage for scene attrs (if mentioned)

references.name: OnOffTransitionTime attribute
references.description: Optional attribute for transition time when null is specified

references.name: DefaultMoveRate attribute
references.description: Optional attribute for default move rate when null is specified

Cluster ID.name: Boolean State
Cluster ID.summary: Provides an interface to a boolean state; if [state] then [true] else [false]; manages [boolean state]

revision_history.description: Initial revision

Classification.summary: Represents a boolean state; if [state is true] then [device is on]; affects [endpoint]

attributes.name: StateValue
attributes.summary: Represents a boolean state; semantics defined by device type

events.name: StateChange
events.summary: If supported, emit when StateValue attribute changes; contains [StateValue]

fields.name: StateValue
fields.summary: Indicates the new value of the StateValue attribute

references.name: ClusterRevision
references.description: Global attr for cluster version (not defined here)

Cluster ID.name: Boolean State Configuration
Cluster ID.summary: Configures boolean state, supports visual/audible alarms, alarm suppression, and sensitivity level settings; manages alarm behavior and reporting (max 200 chars)

revision_history.description: Initial revision

features.name: Visual
features.summary: Supports visual alarms; pseudo: if enabled then [visual alarm supported]; affects [visual alarm behavior]

features.name: Audible
features.summary: Supports audible alarms; pseudo: if enabled then [audible alarm supported]; affects [audible alarm behavior]

features.name: AlarmSuppress
features.summary: Supports ability to suppress or acknowledge alarms; pseudo: if VIS or AUD then [alarm suppression supported]; affects [alarm behavior]

features.name: SensitivityLevel
features.summary: Supports ability to set sensor sensitivity; pseudo: if enabled then [sensor sensitivity configurable]; affects [sensor behavior]

values.name: GeneralFault
values.summary: Unspecified fault detected; pseudo: GeneralFault = [Unspecified fault detected]

attributes.name: CurrentSensitivityLevel
attributes.summary: Indicates the currently selected sensitivity level; on write: if [unsupported value] then return CONSTRAINT_ERROR

attributes.name: SupportedSensitivityLevels
attributes.summary: Indicates the number of supported sensitivity levels by the device

attributes.name: DefaultSensitivityLevel
attributes.summary: Indicates the default sensitivity level selected by the manufacturer

attributes.name: AlarmsActive
attributes.summary: Indicates which specific alarm modes on the server are currently active; when sensor not triggered: attr := 0 for all supported alarm modes

attributes.name: AlarmsSuppressed
attributes.summary: Indicates which specific alarm modes on the server are currently suppressed; when sensor not triggered: attr := 0 for all supported alarm modes

attributes.name: AlarmsEnabled
attributes.summary: Indicates the alarm modes that will be emitted if the sensor is triggered

attributes.name: AlarmsSupported
attributes.summary: Indicates the alarms supported by the sensor

attributes.name: SensorFault
attributes.summary: Indicates any faults registered by the device

commands.name: SuppressAlarm
commands.summary: Suppresses specified alarm modes. pseudo: call SuppressAlarm(AlarmsToSuppress); if alarm_supported and alarm_active then set AlarmsSuppressed := AlarmsToSuppress; affects AlarmsSuppressed

fields.name: AlarmsToSuppress
fields.summary: Indicates the alarm modes to suppress. pseudo: for each alarm: if bit set then suppress alarm

commands.effect_on_receipt: if alarm_mode not supported then return CONSTRAINT_ERROR; else if alarm_mode not active then return INVALID_IN_STATE; else for each valid_bit in AlarmsToSuppress: if alarm already suppressed then ignore bit; else suppress alarm_mode; set AlarmsSuppressed := AlarmsToSuppress

commands.name: EnableDisableAlarm
commands.summary: Enables or disables specified alarm modes. pseudo: call EnableDisableAlarm(AlarmsToEnableDisable); set AlarmsEnabled := AlarmsToEnableDisable; if enabling and trigger met then activate; affects AlarmsEnabled, AlarmsActive

fields.name: AlarmsToEnableDisable
fields.summary: Indicates alarm modes to enable or disable. pseudo: for each alarm: if bit set then enable, else disable

commands.effect_on_receipt: if alarm_mode not supported then return CONSTRAINT_ERROR; else set AlarmsEnabled := AlarmsToEnableDisable; if alarm_mode being enabled and trigger condition met then activate alarm_mode; set AlarmsActive := alarm_mode; else if alarm_mode being disabled then clear alarm_mode; alarm_mode.state := not active

events.name: AlarmsStateChanged
events.summary: Generated after bits in AlarmsActive and/or AlarmsSuppressed attributes change; pseudo: emit when [alarm state changes]; contains [active alarms, suppressed alarms]; triggers [none]

fields.name: AlarmsActive
fields.summary: State of active alarm modes at the time the event was generated; pseudo: contains [AlarmsActive attribute]

fields.name: AlarmsSuppressed
fields.summary: State of suppressed alarm modes at the time the event was generated; pseudo: contains [AlarmsSuppressed attribute]

events.name: SensorFault
events.summary: Generated when the device registers or clears a fault; pseudo: emit when [fault registers/clears]; contains [sensor fault]; triggers [none]

fields.name: SensorFault
fields.summary: Value of the SensorFault attribute at the time this event is generated; pseudo: contains [SensorFault attribute]

references.name: ClusterRevision
references.description: Global attr for cluster version (not defined here)

Cluster ID.name: Mode Select
Cluster ID.summary: Defines distinct purposes for cluster instances, e.g., blinking modes for a light. Supports anonymous and manufacturer-specific semantic tags.

revision_history.description: Initial revision
The MfgCode field was marked non-nullable. Updated the related text. Reorder sections.

features.name: OnOff Dependency
features.summary: Dependency with the OnOff cluster; pseudo: if OnOff cluster is present, then [enable mode control]; affects [mode selection]

attributes.name: Description
attributes.summary: Describes the purpose of the server in readable text.

attributes.name: StandardNamespace
attributes.summary: Indicates a single standard namespace for semantic tags.

attributes.name: SupportedModes
attributes.summary: List of supported modes that may be selected for CurrentMode.

attributes.name: CurrentMode
attributes.summary: Represents the current mode of the server.

attributes.name: StartUpMode
attributes.summary: Desired startup mode for the server when powered up; pseudo: on power up: CurrentMode := StartUpMode

attributes.name: OnMode
attributes.summary: Value of CurrentMode depending on On/Off cluster state; pseudo: on OFF->ON: CurrentMode := OnMode

commands.name: ChangeToMode
commands.summary: Changes the current mode to the specified NewMode. pseudo: set CurrentMode := NewMode; if NewMode is valid then update CurrentMode else respond with INVALID_COMMAND

fields.name: NewMode
fields.summary: The new mode to set. pseudo: if NewMode is valid then set CurrentMode := NewMode

commands.effect_on_receipt: if NewMode is a valid mode transition then set CurrentMode := NewMode else respond with INVALID_COMMAND status

references.name: ClusterRevision
references.description: Global attr for cluster version (not defined here)

references.name: On/Off Cluster
references.description: Dependency cluster for mode control (if mentioned)

references.name: On/Off cluster
references.description: Controls device on/off state; affects CurrentMode via OnMode attribute

Cluster ID.name: Mode Base
Cluster ID.summary: Manages device modes and their dependencies, potentially linked to OnOff cluster. See OnMode for more information. (placeholder ID)

revision_history.description: Initial revision
ChangeToModeResponse command: StatusText must be provided for InvalidInMode status. Require at least one standard mode tag. Define reserved ranges for base/derived clusters

Classification.summary: Base application cluster affecting endpoint behavior.

features.name: OnOff Dependency
features.summary: Dependency with the OnOff cluster; pseudo: if OnOff cluster is on same endpoint then [enable mode base]; affects [mode selection]

attributes.name: SupportedModes
attributes.summary: List of supported modes that may be selected for CurrentMode.

attributes.name: CurrentMode
attributes.summary: Indicates the current mode of the server; may change via out-of-band interaction.

attributes.name: StartUpMode
attributes.summary: Desired startup mode for the server when supplied with power; pseudo: on power up: CurrentMode := StartUpMode

attributes.name: OnMode
attributes.summary: CurrentMode depends on OnOff cluster state; pseudo: if OnOff=ON then CurrentMode := OnMode

commands.name: ChangeToMode
commands.summary: Changes device modes; pseudo: call ChangeToMode(NewMode); affects CurrentMode attribute

fields.name: NewMode
fields.summary: The new mode to switch to; pseudo: set CurrentMode := NewMode

commands.effect_on_receipt: if NewMode not in SupportedModes then set Status := UnsupportedMode; set StatusText := "error string"; else if device cannot transition then set Status := product-specific error; set StatusText := "error string"; else set CurrentMode := NewMode; set Status := Success; set StatusText := "optional string"; if NewMode == CurrentMode then set Status := Success; set StatusText := "optional string"; call ChangeToModeResponse(Status, StatusText)

commands.name: ChangeToModeResponse
commands.summary: Response to ChangeToMode command; pseudo: return Status, StatusText

fields.name: Status
fields.summary: Status of the mode change request; pseudo: return status code

fields.name: StatusText
fields.summary: Human-readable status description; pseudo: return error string

commands.effect_on_receipt: N/A

ranges.name: CommonTags
ranges.summary: Common standard values defined in this cluster specification

ranges.name: DerivedClusterTags
ranges.summary: Derived cluster specific standard values defined in the derived cluster specification

ranges.name: MfgTags
ranges.summary: Manufacturer-specific values. For derived cluster instances, manufacturer specific under the derived cluster

common_tags.name: Auto
common_tags.summary: Device decides which options, features and setting values to use

common_tags.name: Quick
common_tags.summary: The mode of the device is optimizing for faster completion

common_tags.name: Quiet
common_tags.summary: The device is silent or barely audible while in this mode

common_tags.name: LowNoise
common_tags.summary: Either the mode is inherently low noise or the device optimizes for that

common_tags.name: LowEnergy
common_tags.summary: The device is optimizing for lower energy usage in this mode (Eco mode)

common_tags.name: Vacation
common_tags.summary: A mode suitable for use during vacations or other extended absences

common_tags.name: Min
common_tags.summary: The mode uses the lowest available setting value

common_tags.name: Max
common_tags.summary: The mode uses the highest available setting value

common_tags.name: Night
common_tags.summary: The mode is recommended or suitable for use during night time

common_tags.name: Day
common_tags.summary: The mode is recommended or suitable for use during day time

references.name: ClusterRevision
references.description: Global attr for cluster version (not defined here)

references.name: On/Off cluster
references.description: Determines CurrentMode if OnMode attribute is present; if OnOff=ON then CurrentMode := OnMode

Cluster ID.name: Low Power
Cluster ID.summary: Manages low power mode on a device; provides sleep() command to manually put the device into low power mode; manages device power state.

revision_history.description: Initial revision

commands.name: Sleep
commands.summary: Puts the device into low power mode; pseudo: set device.power := low; affects device.power
commands.effect_on_receipt: set device.power := low

references.name: ClusterRevision
references.description: Global attr for cluster version (not defined here)

references.name: Wake On LAN
references.description: External protocol for waking up devices
